Senior iOS Engineer

Prism Labs — Crafting intuitive mobile experiences that connect people through technology

Location

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$160,000 - $210,000 per year

The Opportunity

Prism Labs is seeking a Senior iOS Engineer with 5-8 years of professional iOS development

experience to lead the technical implementation of our flagship health and wellness application. You'll

architect robust, scalable iOS solutions using modern Swift and SwiftUI frameworks, mentor junior

developers, and collaborate with product and design teams to deliver high-quality user experiences.

This role offers significant technical ownership and the opportunity to shape the future of our mobile

platform.

About Prism Labs

Prism Labs is a fast-growing mobile-first technology company founded in 2018, headquartered in San
Francisco. We specialize in building consumer-facing iOS applications that prioritize user experience
and performance. Our products serve over 5 million active users across health, productivity, and

social domains. We're committed to creating cutting-edge mobile solutions that solve real problems for
our users.

Day-to-Day

Design and implement advanced iOS features using Swift, SwiftUI, and UIKit, making informed

architectural decisions that balance modern frameworks with legacy support

Lead the migration of legacy UIKit components to SwiftUI, establishing best practices and
mentoring junior engineers through the transition

Architect and maintain complex data persistence layers using Core Data and modern alternatives,
ensuring optimal performance and data integrity

Implement and optimize reactive programming patterns using Combine framework for event
handling and asynchronous operations

Conduct code reviews and provide technical mentorship to mid-level and junior iOS developers,
fostering a culture of excellence and knowledge sharing

Collaborate with backend engineers to design efficient APIs and integrate with multiple third-party

SDKs and services

Optimize app performance, memory usage, and battery consumption, using Instruments and

Xcode profiling tools to identify and resolve bottlenecks

Manage end-to-end App Store deployment processes, including submission workflows, versioning,
and handling App Store Connect reviews and compliance issues

 Participate in architectural discussions and contribute to long-term technical strategy for the iOS

platform

You Have

5-8 years of professional iOS development experience with a strong track record of shipped apps

in the App Store

Expert-level proficiency in Swift and deep understanding of object-oriented and functional

programming paradigms

Demonstrated experience with both UIKit and SwiftUI, including modern concurrency patterns and

async/await

Strong knowledge of Core Data or other persistence frameworks, with experience optimizing

complex data models

Proven ability to lead technical initiatives, architect solutions, and mentor junior developers

Solid understanding of iOS memory management, performance optimization, and debugging using

Xcode and Instruments

Experience with Combine framework and reactive programming patterns in production applications

Bonus

Experience with Swift Concurrency (async/await, actors) in production applications

Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and automated testing frameworks for iOS (XCTest, UI testing)

Knowledge of accessibility (VoiceOver, Dynamic Type) and inclusive design principles

Previous experience mentoring or leading a small team of iOS engineers

Our Stack

Swift   SwiftUI   UIKit   Xcode   Combine   Core Data   Instruments   App Store Connect   XCTest   Async/Await

What We Offer

Competitive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% premium coverage

Flexible work schedule and remote-work options with optional office presence

$5,000 annual professional development and learning budget

Generous paid time off: 20 days PTO + 10 company holidays + 5 sick days

Stock options and quarterly performance bonuses

Free lunch and snacks in the office, plus DoorDash credits for remote days

Mental health support including subsidized therapy and wellness programs

MacBook Pro (16") and Monitor stipend for equipment of choice

Apply

To apply, please submit your resume, a portfolio link or GitHub profile showcasing your iOS projects,
and a brief cover letter explaining your interest in the role. Apply directly through our careers portal at

 prismlabs.com/careers or email your application to ios-careers@prismlabs.com. We review

applications on a rolling basis and will contact qualified candidates within one week.

Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and automated testing frameworks for iOS (XCTest, UI testing)
Knowledge of accessibility (VoiceOver, Dynamic Type) and inclusive design principles
Previous experience mentoring or leading a small team of iOS engineers
